## [00:00 - 02:32]

Okay, one of the rituals, one of your rituals has to be call calculations. Look, there's going to be a day where you get on that phone and you don't sell anything. You can be like, oh, it was a disaster day. No, it wasn't. You think it was a disaster because you didn't sell a deal, you didn't get an appointment. It was not a disaster. Every call counts. What most people don't do is they haven't done the call calculation or you did it once. You did it a bunch of segments ago. Oh, that was a good idea. Call calculations are valuable. Time is money. Effort, effort. The only reason a person fails is because they underestimate the amount of energy and effort required to get something to pay off. Right? If I shovel enough times sooner or later, I dig a hole, period. The guy goes out there, digs a hole. Oh, I didn't get to where I'm going. Because he quit. He doesn't understand the call calculations, okay? If you're going to dig three holes, make three phone calls, 30 phone calls, 300 phone calls, those are worth something. Right? You understand? I know the more calls I make, the closer I get to that prize. And I also know this. The number of calls, you should have a calculation knowing the number of calls sooner or later equals money. So let's say I make 100 phone calls and I produce $10,000. That means those calls are worth whatever $100,000 into $10,000 is. What is that? Like $100 each? Right? The 80th call paid me $10,000. The 80th call paid me $10,000. But the truth is, 100 calls paid me $10,000. You get it? So every call, every call was worth $100. So I go home at night. I'm like, I made 225 phone calls today, right? I didn't make any money. Didn't make a sale. Didn't make an appointment. But I know this. Over time, I will make $22,500 if I did the math right there. Okay? That's why you want to do your call calculation. What is every call worth? Even the ones that appear not to pay you today. Oh, by the way, every call I make today is going to be followed up with another call, another text, another email, a link. It builds out my funnel. You're disappointed and discouraged at the end of the day because your funnel is too small. Every call I make today should be making that funnel bigger. These are investments, investments in the future. Like you're investing in a stock market, you're investing in your future. It's guaranteed to pay you off. It's guaranteed you're in control of this company called you. The phone is your equipment and call calculation is one of the tools you need in a daily ritual you need to commit to.
